title: #architecture: 7088881234
preview:
Nora Blake: Quick sync — I want to propose a compact contract for route lifecycle that reduces ambiguity between policy authors and the runtime. Too many incidents from routes disappearing.
Ethan Park: +1. High level: policy expresses intent; runtime enforces safety. We need explicit fields and a clear clamp/audit mechanism.
Maya Torres: Suggested minimal data model: `intent_expiry_seconds` (nullable), `soft_deprecate_seconds`, `prune_after_seconds`. Runtime exposes `safety_cap_seconds` per deployment and may shorten any intent but must log `consensus_clamp` with `actor` and `reason`.
Sven Müller: For private/on-prem installs add signed intent bundles. Control plane signs; local admin can sign for air-gapped installs. Runtime validates signature and rejects expired bundles.
Nora Blake: Coordinator question: who schedules prunes? Control plane preferred for hosted customers; local runtime for local-only customers. We should support both but keep canonical precedence: control-plane > local.
Ethan Park: Implementation detail: when control-plane is present it publishes a `prune-plan` in batches. Runtimes can `dry-run` the plan and report back before enforcement. Dry-run mode also helps private customers validate.
Maya Torres: Telemetry contract: events `route.intent_declared`, `route.intent_clamped`, `route.soft_deprecated`, `route.pruned`. Fields: policy_id, route_id, declared_ttl, effective_ttl, clamp_reason, runtime_version, deployment_mode. Keep payload small.
Sven Müller: API ergonomics: add `GET /v1/policies/{id}/prune-preview?window=72h` and CLI `redwood route preview --policy-id`. Also runtime should surface header `X-Redwood-Route-Deprecation: soft;eta=2026-03-12T12:00:00Z` during soft-deprecate window.
Nora Blake: Linting rules: reject `ttl: 0` unless `permanent: true`. Warn when legacy `ttl` used; require new policies to include `intent_*` fields. Add auto-fix suggestion in CI.
Ethan Park: Emergency ops: `operator_override` endpoint that is timeboxed (auto-expire 1h) and requires an attached post-mortem ticket. Overrides must appear in telemetry as `operator_override:<id>`.
Maya Torres: Migration plan: 1) accept both legacy `ttl` and new `intent_*` fields and emit warnings; 2) enable linter enforcement for PRs; 3) after grace period start enforcing clamps only on new routes, then migrate existing routes via console-assisted flow.
Sven Müller: Testing: add `route-lifecycle-sim` harness to exercise active->soft->prune transitions, signed-bundle verification tests, and clamp audit propagation. Include integration test that validates header emission and audit events.
Nora Blake: Rollout: feature-flag `route-intent-lifecycle-v2`. Start internal, then opt-in beta, then default. Provide a clear opt-out for enterprise/private customers.
Ethan Park: Docs checklist: data model (examples), CLI commands, migration steps, operator runbook for overrides, and signed-bundle key rotation guide for private customers.
Maya Torres: Action items: I'll draft the schema + linter PR. Sven prototype telemetry and signed-bundle flow. Ethan draft preview API + CLI. OpsBot: create epic for rollout.
OpsBot: Epic created RW-PLT-9001 "Intent-first route lifecycle & prune plan". Assigned owners and initial milestones.
Nora Blake: Revisit in 4 working days after PRs land. Thanks everyone. :thumbsup:

title: #docs: 4101234567
preview:
Tomiko Sato: I opened PR to bundle a tiny rate/quotas "cheatsheet" for quick customers + a migration note for the console rename. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/981
Ethan Park: Nice. Can we make the cheatsheet one pager: headers list, minimal cross-language snippets, and a short troubleshooting box? Keep it copy-paste friendly.
Tomiko Sato: That's the plan. Will include: X-Quota-Tier, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset, X-Burst-Credits, X-Usage-Cost and legacy mappings (x-old-quota, x-old-burst).
Rosa Delgado: For screenshots: console panel id changed -> panelId=88. Add both links and mark the old one as deprecated. I'll upload new PNGs to the branch.
Will Freeman: Snippets request: tiny curl, Python (sync), TS (async), and a 5-line Go fallback example. No heavy explanations, quick code only.
Tomiko Sato: Draft curl example for the cheatsheet:
```bash
curl -i -H "Authorization: Bearer $REDWOOD_KEY" https://api.redwood.ai/v2/generate | grep -i "X-Quota-Tier\|X-Burst-Credits\|X-Usage-Cost"
```
Ethan Park: Small nit: grep output differs on mac vs linux. Add a one-line note: "If grep behaves differently on macOS, use `-i` and explicitly test with `curl -I`."
Tomiko Sato: Added note. Draft Python cost calc snippet (2 lines):
```python
est = lambda tokens, p=0.0025: round(tokens/1000 * p, 4)
print(est(1530))
```
Rosa Delgado: For migration note: "If your Dedicated telemetry proxy is older than v2.1.3, map `x-old-quota` -> `X-Quota-Tier` and `x-old-burst` -> `X-Burst-Credits` or upgrade proxy." Keep it one sentence.
Will Freeman: Go fallback snippet (tiny):
```go
credits, _ := strconv.Atoi(resp.Header.Get("X-Burst-Credits"))
if credits < 10 { useModel("gpt-economy") }
```
Tomiko Sato: Added a short troubleshooting box: missing X-Usage-Cost -> compute client-side using published model rates; unexpected tier -> clear proxy cache and check telemetry-proxy config.
i18n-bot: Heads up: keep old panel label in parentheses for 14 days to minimize translator churn. Please add TODO with removal date in the markdown.
Tomiko Sato: Done, added TODO: remove old label 14 days after merge. Also added alt text for new screenshots per Rosa's upload.
DocsBot: PR 981 opened by Tomiko Sato — CI: docs-build (staging) passed ✅ — Preview: https://deploy-preview-981--docs.redwood.ai
Ethan Park: QA checklist on the PR: update screenshots, validate all header names with eng-runtime, check snippets on mac/linux, add changelog line `docs: rate/quota cheatsheet + migration note`, and tag i18n. I'll review tomorrow morning.
Tomiko Sato: Thanks — aiming to merge Thu EOD. Will post a short announce blurb after merge. :sparkles:

title: #docs: 3865432100
preview:
Ethan Park: CI spotted a couple of strange broken fragments after merging the SDK examples PR. I see `/guides/sdk#client-usage` -> generated `#/guides/sdk#client-usage%EF%BB%BF`.
Sofia Liu: %EF%BB%BF is BOM (UTF-8 BOM) getting appended? did the codeblock conversion add a file header by accident?
Ethan Park: checking the diff, the example file `docs/guides/sdk.md` had a fenced snippet pasted from Windows editor that included a BOM at the start of the fenced block, and our snippet exporter kept it when building the demo heading.
Ravi Patel: quick fix: strip BOM from snippet content during rendering. longer-term: add a precommit or a docs-lint rule that rejects non-printable or BOM characters in markdown source.
Sofia Liu: agree. I can open a small PR to sanitize snippet text in `mdx-snippets/renderer.js` (call `.replace(/^\uFEFF/, '')` on snippet source) and add a unit test.
Ethan Park: please also run a grep to find other BOMs: `grep -rl $'\xEF\xBB\xBF' docs/ || true` and add redirects for any anchor changes we find.
docs-bot: CI: docs/linkcheck failed on branch fix/strip-bom \u2014 2 broken fragments: docs/guides/sdk.md (line 52), docs/examples/quickstart.md (line 14)
Sofia Liu: found two files with BOMs; adding the sanitization change and a tiny redirect mapping for `/guides/sdk#client-usage%EF%BB%BF` -> `/guides/sdk#client-usage`.
Ravi Patel: opened PR #4991 with the BOM strip + test and a docs-lint rule that fails on control chars (regex matching [\x00-\x1F\x7F\xA0]). added explanation in CONTRIBUTING.md about not pasting from Windows editors without cleaning.
Ethan Park: ran local `yarn docs:linkcheck` after patch, all green. pushed and CC'd QA for a quick smoke deploy.
docs-bot: CI: docs/build-staging succeeded for PR #4991 \u2014 all checks passed :white_check_mark:
Ravi Patel: merged. follow-up: Sofia to run a repo-wide BOM search and remove any remaining occurrences; Ethan to monitor link logs for one deployment cycle.
Sofia Liu: will run the search and open cleanup PRs if any leftovers. thanks team.
Ethan Park: nice. this should reduce noisy 404s from bookmarks that include weird encodings.

title: #eng-infra: 3301239999
preview:
Ethan Park: Heads-up: USW1 rack-11 showing repeated p95 spikes and a thermal uptick since 01:08. Looks like a single tenant doing aggressive multi-stream prefetch + long decode.

Rina Sato: telemetry matches. NIC bursts and HBM pinned. Which tenant?

Ethan Park: tid-4147 (partner eval). No dedicated carve. InfraBot alerted at 01:10.

InfraBot: ALERT rack-11: gpu_temp=83C, nic_tx=920MB/s, slab_evictions +340%. Ticket INC-10112 created.

Samir Khan: short-term mitigation: soft-fence into noisy-pool + apply egress policer and reduce concurrency. We have knobs: --egress_kbps and --max_streams.

Ava Lopez: please run conservative values first so we don't break the job: egress 650000 kbps, max_streams=2.

Samir Khan: running now:
```
allocatorctl set-tenant-policy --tenant=tid-4147 --pool=noisy-rack-pool --egress_kbps=650000 --max_streams=2
```
InfraBot: ACTION applied.

(45s) InfraBot: policy applied. Monitoring link: https://telemetry.redwood/rack-11/INC-10112

Rina Sato: immediate effect: egress down ~30%, tail p95 improving. KV evictions dropping but still ~1.4x baseline.

Ethan Park: propose automation: if slab_evictions > 250% for 10m post-soft-fence -> schedule fractional carveout + 1hr migration window and notify Billing.

Samir Khan: SGTM. Also logging tid-4147 for post-incident customer advisory: recommend smaller prefetch windows, KV hinting, or short-term reserved slice for sustained throughput.

Ava Lopez: will draft advisory and ops runbook step. Closing thread after 30m stable. :thumbsup:

title: #customer-success: 3288881234
preview:
Ethan Park (Orbit): Hey CS, onboarding hiccup. After ~75s our service started seeing a 403 followed by a burst of 429s. Logs say 'token_invalid' then 'quota_exceeded: per_key'. Demo in 48h, can we triage?
Priya Khatri: Thanks Ethan. Which SDK/version and are you routing through a proxy or direct to API?
Ethan Park (Orbit): Node SDK v2.2.0 behind our API gateway. We mint short tokens TTL=120s, rotate 25s before expiry, and keep prior token for 50s so streams don't drop.
Jordan Ruiz: Keeping the previous token for 50s is likely the culprit if your gateway multiplexes connections. Two distinct tokens hitting auth in quick succession can count twice against the per-key quota.
Ethan Park (Orbit): Repro log snippet: 
# t=0 -> 200 OK
# t=80 token swap -> 403 {"error":"token_invalid"}
# immediate retries -> 429 {"error":"quota_exceeded","quota":"per_key"}
Jordan Ruiz: Two things to try: 1) reduce old-token retention to <=5s for a quick validation, 2) increase retry backoff to 150-250ms to avoid tight retry bursts.
Priya Khatri: We can also temporarily relax per-key window for Orbit while you validate. If the quick test passes we'll update onboarding docs to call out header-buffering behavior behind multiplexing proxies.
CS Bot: Ticket created: CS-9123  labels: onboarding, auth, rate-quota. Temp relax enabled until 3288884999.
Ethan Park (Orbit): Will try old-token keep=3s + backoff=200ms now, will report back in ~15m. Thanks.
Jordan Ruiz: If you use long streaming, prefer using the same token for stream lifetime and only rotate on new connections to avoid mid-stream swaps.
Priya Khatri: Follow-up: if the test is green we'll push a short playbook entry and a reproduce script for customers.
Ethan Park (Orbit): Quick test success  10m no 403/429 under ramp. Demo looks safe for now. Will confirm after wider ramp.
Priya Khatri: Great. Action items: Ethan to continue ramp and confirm, CS to add playbook note, eng to attach logs to CS-9123 and close when stable. Thanks all. :tada:

title: #eng-ml: 3129988776
preview:
Ethan Park: FYI I hit a reproducible divergence in the new int4-qkernel during a long-context smoke: mismatch appears when absolute position indices exceed ~100k.
Sofia Li: Can you share the repro command and a small log?
Ethan Park: repro: `python test/long_kv_check.py --model builds/int4-alpha --prompt long/100k.txt --dump-meta`
Ethan Park: log snip:
[2026-02-11 09:14:02] PREFILL tokens=100128
[2026-02-11 09:14:02] KV_WRITE pos=100128 shard=1 hdr.pos_lo=46768
[2026-02-11 09:14:03] KV_READ reconstructed_pos=46768 (expected=100129)
Rohan Desai: hdr.pos_lo looks truncated; likely compact header using 16-bit. Is this new packing introduced in qkernel v2?
Ethan Park: yes, the qkernel team packed header to save memory. FP16 path not affected.
Sofia Li: Quick mitigation: runtime flag `kv.header_mode=full32` to force 32-bit positions for canary. Add unit test: for x in [0, 200000]: assert(unpack(pack(x))==x).
Rohan Desai: Also test interaction with RoPE scaling: setting rotary_scale=1.25 moves the failure point earlier. That suggests the effective index used in packing is scaled before packing.
build-bot: CI job eng-ml/kv-long/3129988776 queued
Ethan Park: Filed ticket ENG-ML-7421 "int4 compact header truncation under large priming + RoPE" with repro and logs. Planning emergency canary with full32 header and regression eval (ppl/bleu on 100k set).
Sofia Li: Thanks. Add to onboarding checklist: "run long-meta header sanity test" before merging new quant kernels.
Ethan Park: CI failed and reproduced locally. Landing emergency patch and scheduling kernel triage.

title: #docs: 2078901234
preview:
Priya Singh: hey folks — opening a tiny PR to tidy the hello-world quickstart: standardize env var to REDWOOD_KEY, add PowerShell examples, and adjust Dockerfile ordering so `npm ci` caches. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/2533 :wave:
Ethan Park: thanks. can you paste the minimal migration snippet you'll include? want to make sure ops teams can grep and update infra templates without surprises.
Priya Singh: sure, will add a short table and a two-line diff. diff for python/node users:

```diff
- import redwood
- client = redwood.Client(api_key=os.environ['OLD_KEY'])
+ from @redwood/inference import Redwood
+ client = Redwood({ "apiKey": process.env.REDWOOD_KEY })
```

Maya Flores: please keep a python example too (requests). lots of users start with py. also add the curl PS vs bash variants.
Priya Singh: done — examples will be three variants. bash curl:

```bash
curl -H "x-api-key: $REDWOOD_KEY" -d '{"input":"hello"}' https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate
```

PowerShell (Invoke-RestMethod):

```powershell
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate -Method Post -Headers @{"x-api-key" = $env:REDWOOD_KEY} -Body (@{input = "hello"} | ConvertTo-Json)
```

Python requests:

```python
import os, requests
resp = requests.post("https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate", headers={"x-api-key": os.environ.get("REDWOOD_KEY")}, json={"input":"hello"})
print(resp.json())
```

Ethan Park: on Docker, small change: copy package.json first, run npm ci, then copy rest. keeps dependencies cached. snippet I used:

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --production
FROM node:20-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
CMD ["node","server.js"]
```

Maya Flores: add a short troubleshooting table? 401 -> missing key/typo; 403 -> key scope; 429 -> rate limit. link to Console > API keys.
Priya Singh: yup adding Troubleshooting and linking Console. also adding a collapsible "SDK migration" note so quickstart stays short.
build-bot: PR 2533 — site preview started.

text:
Priya Singh: hey folks — opening a tiny PR to tidy the hello-world quickstart: standardize env var to REDWOOD_KEY, add PowerShell examples, and adjust Dockerfile ordering so `npm ci` caches. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/2533 :wave:
Ethan Park: thanks. can you paste the minimal migration snippet you'll include? want to make sure ops teams can grep and update infra templates without surprises.
Priya Singh: sure, will add a short table and a two-line diff. diff for python/node users:

```diff
- import redwood
- client = redwood.Client(api_key=os.environ['OLD_KEY'])
+ from @redwood/inference import Redwood
+ client = Redwood({ "apiKey": process.env.REDWOOD_KEY })
```

Maya Flores: please keep a python example too (requests). lots of users start with py. also add the curl PS vs bash variants.
Priya Singh: done — examples will be three variants. bash curl:

```bash
curl -H "x-api-key: $REDWOOD_KEY" -d '{"input":"hello"}' https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate
```

PowerShell (Invoke-RestMethod):

```powershell
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate -Method Post -Headers @{"x-api-key" = $env:REDWOOD_KEY} -Body (@{input = "hello"} | ConvertTo-Json)
```

Python requests:

```python
import os, requests
resp = requests.post("https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate", headers={"x-api-key": os.environ.get("REDWOOD_KEY")}, json={"input":"hello"})
print(resp.json())
```

Ethan Park: on Docker, small change: copy package.json first, run npm ci, then copy rest. keeps dependencies cached. snippet I used:

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --production
FROM node:20-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
CMD ["node","server.js"]
```

Maya Flores: add a short troubleshooting table? 401 -> missing key/typo; 403 -> key scope; 429 -> rate limit. link to Console > API keys.
Priya Singh: yup adding Troubleshooting and linking Console. also adding a collapsible "SDK migration" note so quickstart stays short.

title: #customer-success: 2072103456
preview:
Priya Rao: Morning — quick heads-up on the Jade account dedicated pool. Noticed a saturation spike overnight, sustained around ~03:10 UTC. High-level: GPU hours up ~42% WoW, queue depth peaked at 32, spend is trending higher. Can ops/eng confirm root cause and whether this will affect the renewal convo later this week? Attaching the alert logs below.

ops-bot: ```ALERT: pool=dedicated-jade saturation=98% at 2026-03-20T03:10:22Z
queue_depth=32
95p_latency_ms=290
error_rate_pct=0.4
top_consumer=jade-analytics-job-9f2b```

Luca Moretti: On it — found a long-running batch (jade-analytics-job-9f2b) that kicked off at ~02:40 UTC and kept grabbing slots. It wasn't configured as low-priority so it displaced inference traffic. I throttled replicas and set `priorityClass=low` for the remaining runs. Current pool status: saturation ~63%, queue depth 3.
24h GPU hours: 72h (baseline ~50–52h). Estimated incremental spend ~ $820/day. Snippet from the pool metrics:```pool:deds-jade saturation:63%
queue_depth:3
gpu_hours_24h:72
cost_delta_per_day:820```

Ethan Park: Did we see customer-facing latency impact? Jade has a renewal call Friday — we need numbers for the deck.

Luca Moretti: Yes — 95p for `/jade/api/generate` rose from ~210ms -> ~290ms during the peak window. Error rate for that route nudged up ~0.2 percentage points. We pushed a portion of traffic to a smaller model (`redwood-small-v2`) as an emergency fallback which prevented 95p going above ~400ms.

Zoe Chen: Noted. For the renewal I'd recommend: 1) offer a one-time credit (proposal: 20% of this month's dedicated overage) to smooth the conversation; 2) run an optimization workshop with Eng/runtime to tune batch scheduling and autoscale; 3) add preemptible scheduling for heavy analytics jobs so they don't compete with low-latency inference. Also capture feedback: they said the scheduling docs were a bit sparse. :eyes: :calendar:

Priya Rao: Action items — Luca: file a PR to enforce `priorityClass=low` for `jade-analytics` by EOD; Ethan: prepare a 1-pager with latency impact and mitigations for the renewal deck; Zoe: draft the credit proposal and propose two workshop slots (Thu AM or Mon next week). I'll loop in the AM success lead and copy @eng-runtime for the preemptible scheduling ask.

ops-bot: ```STATUS: mitigation applied, saturation down to 63%, queue depth down to 3. Monitor window 1h.```

Priya Rao: Thanks team — keeping this thread as the single source for the renewal notes. If anyone sees a re-spike tag this thread and DM me.

preview:
Nadia Ahmed: Heads up: HarborFlow (mid-market, renewal in 5d) flagged at-risk. Low adoption of scheduled jobs, reported spikes in token cost after a recent feature toggle. They mentioned timeouts on some batch exports. Need a concise save play we can run before renewal.
Ethan Park: Any telemetry links? Is it a new integration or change in usage patterns?
Riley Morgan: Quick look: new webhook consumer enabled last week; requests are long (avg 12k tokens) and some retries loop. Also their feature toggle accidentally set verbose logging -> extra tokens.
ops-bot: Alert: HarborFlow export retries detected, duplicate rate 6k/hr. See incident HF-2211.
Samir Desai: Two parallel tracks: immediate mitigation (rate-limit + toggle revert), and a short commercial/enablement offer for reassurance. Recommend 10-day trial credits + engineer support.
Jules Kim: I'll draft a short customer message and an enablement checklist. We should offer a scoped temporary SLA on export route if they accept remediation.
Ethan Park: Technical fixes I can push now: 1) revert verbose logging setting, 2) apply a temporary per-route throttle (300 r/s), 3) enable token cap for export endpoint (max_tokens=4000). All reversible.
Riley Morgan: I'll prepare a preload for common prefixes and a quick script to dedupe webhook retries. Also will surface p95 latency before/after.
Nadia Ahmed: Commercial: propose 2-week 20% credit + two 1-hour enablement sessions. If they commit to a 3-month reserved plan within 30d, convert credits to prepaid. Also include opt to keep one critical route on reserved capacity at current price.
Samir Desai: If they push to downgrade, use a graded downgrade: reduce non-customer-facing exports to best-effort while keeping storefront and billing routes on standard SLA. Capture that in a downgrade matrix.
Jules Kim: Draft message:
``
Hi HarborFlow team,
We saw increased export sizes and some retry loops after your recent toggle. Immediate steps we propose: (1) 30-min triage call, (2) revert verbose logging and apply temporary throttle, (3) 2-week cost credit while we validate. We can also enable a temporary SLA for the export route during the trial.
Thanks, Nadia + Redwood CS
```
Ethan Park: Reverted toggle and applied throttle. Monitoring shows duplicate rate dropped ~92%. Will keep sampling.
Riley Morgan: Dedupe script staged; ready for prod push after call. Preload script ready too.
Nadia Ahmed: Owners & deadlines:
- Jules: customer email + one-pager (owner) - due in 4h
- Ethan: toggles & throttles (owner) - done
- Riley: dedupe + preload + metrics (owner) - push after call
- Samir: commercial terms & downgrade matrix (owner) - draft in 6h
Call scheduled for 9am PT tomorrow. If no agreement within 48h post-call, escalate to @Head_CS.
Samir Desai: Draft uploaded to shared drive: https://drive.redwood/hf-save-1952. Added downgrade matrix and conversion incentives.
Jules Kim: One-pager and email sent to Nadia for review. Also included a short NPS + friction survey to run after trial.
Nadia Ahmed: Great work. If call successful, target conversion to a 3-month reserved uplift. If they instead choose downgrade, follow the staged downgrade to preserve revenue-critical routes. Tag updates here.

title: #customer-success: 1940129876
preview:
Maya Chen: FYI — I'm starting a proactive perf check for Larkspur (onboarding account). Seeing p95 ~740ms on gen calls and unit cost ~ $12 / 1M tokens. Renewal conversation in ~9 weeks, flagging as moderate risk if we don't show improvements.
Ethan Park: Can you paste the current model + runtime config?
Maya Chen: model=redwood/long-llm-7b @v2, quantization=none, region=us-west-2, batching=1, kv_cache=disabled, routing=default-tiered
Ravi Iyer: Quick read: we've seen this profile before. My recommendation: 1) swap to rq4g-v2 (4-bit quant) as a canary, 2) enable per-session KV prefix caching, 3) route lower-priority analytic calls to spot/dedicated-cheap pool in us-west-2, 4) do 10% canary -> 50% rollout with automatic rollback on quality delta >1.5%
Maya Chen: Ran a small sim on their prompt set — rq4g-v2 numbers look promising: p95 drops ~740ms -> ~420ms, median latency shaved ~35%, estimated token cost -28%. Note: we saw occasional tokenization variance on >1k tokens.
Jo Martinez: Customer ask from last call: they need transcripts to be near-deterministic for billing downstream. Any expected regressions there with 4-bit quant? We should capture that in the eval.
Ethan Park: I'll create a focused eval harness: golden prompt set (50 examples) + metrics: exact-match for critical fields, embedding drift, and semantic similarity. Will run baseline (v2) vs rq4g-v2 and post results here. ETA: 24h.
Ravi Iyer: Rollout snippet for ops (example):
```
routing:
  - rule: low_priority_to_spot
    match: request.tags.priority == 'low'
    target_pool: us-west-2-spot
model_override:
  base: redwood/long-llm-7b@v2
  canary: rq4g-v2
canary: 0.10
quality_monitor:
  delta_threshold: 0.015
  check_set: larkspur-golden-50
```
Maya Chen: I'll coordinate the enablement session with Larkspur — proposing Wed 11am PT. Jo can you own scheduling and invite SE? @Jo Martinez
Jo Martinez: Got it, I'll send invite and include a short agenda: what changed, expected perf delta, QA checklist, rollback plan.
Ethan Park: Uploaded initial eval run: baseline -> rq4g-v2 diff: EM drop 0.8%, embedding cosine avg 0.992, hallucination flags +0.2%. Attaching full CSV to workspace link: https://redwood.workspaces/s/teams/larkspur/evals/1940129876
Maya Chen: Nice. Given those deltas, ROI looks good. I'll draft the customer-facing note: perf changes, small QA caveat, suggested canary window of 2 days.
deploy-bot: Canary deployment started for project:larkspur canary_id:can-1940129876 at 1940131200
Ravi Iyer: Monitoring hooks: added trace for kv-hit-rate and model-variance. Alert if kv-hit < 30% or p95 increases above baseline.
Jo Martinez: After enablement we'll capture feedback items in the renewal playbook and route any feature asks to product.
Maya Chen: Action items: 1) Ethan — finalize eval & share detailed report (due +24h); 2) Jo — schedule enablement (due tomorrow); 3) Ravi — apply routing + monitoring canary (done); 4) Maya — send customer note post-canary. Assign and ack here.
Ethan Park: Ack.
Jo Martinez: Ack.
Ravi Iyer: Ack.
Maya Chen: :thumbsup:
deploy-bot: Canary health check passed at 1940132300 (p95=410ms, kv-hit=47%, quality-delta=0.9%)
Maya Chen: Thanks team — will update account health dashboard and flag improvement to Renewal lead.
Jo Martinez: I'll capture the customer's feedback during enablement and add to renewal risk tracker.
Maya Chen: Closing thread for now, will re-open if canary needs rollback or customer escalates.

Priya Desai: Default SLA for Hosted is 99.9. For Dedicated we can offer 99.95 with proper capacity commitments. Multi-region failover is an add-on & requires solutions engagement.

title: #new-hires: 1797709999
preview:
Ethan Park: hi team, im ethan — first day in infra eng. Trying to run a local integration that needs `kubectl port-forward` to `dev-proxy` but i get "error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)" even after merging the quickstart kubeconfig. Any pointers?

onboard-bot: Welcome Ethan! Please file the Kube onboarding request if you haven't: https://go.redwood.internal/onboard-kube. Share the request ID here so infra can fast-track.

devex: hey ethan, welcome! quick checks: 1) which kubectl version? `kubectl version --client --short` 2) `kubectl config current-context` and `kubectl auth can-i portforward pods -n dev-ethan` 3) if you merged multiple kubeconfigs try `KUBECONFIG=~/.kube/config kubectl config view --raw | grep -n context`

Ethan Park: kubectl version --client --short -> Client Version: v1.27.2
current-context -> none
can-i -> Error from server (Forbidden): users "system:anonymous" cannot "create" resource "pods/portforward" in API group "" in the namespace "dev-ethan"

eng-infra: thanks, sounds like your kubeconfig isn't authenticating against the cluster (contexts empty). We can create a short-lived service account for your sandbox. Please DM your work email and confirm you'll need port-forward + `edit` in namespace `dev-ethan` only. We'll issue a kubeconfig that expires in 6 days.

sre-lee: fyi @eng-infra — dev-ethan is in the dev-sandboxes pool. We allow port-forward/edit for new hires if the request is tracked. For anything that needs secret access we do separate approval.

onboard-bot: Request created: KUBE-REQ-2399. Link: https://go.redwood.internal/requests/KUBE-REQ-2399

eng-infra: done — uploaded a service-account kubeconfig to Vault at https://vault.redwood/internal/kubeconfigs/KUBE-REQ-2399. Bound role `edit` in `dev-ethan` and added group `new-hires-2026`. Try this: `export KUBECONFIG=/tmp/ethan-kube && kubectl get svc -n dev-ethan` then `kubectl port-forward -n dev-ethan svc/dev-proxy 8080:80`

Ethan Park: small hiccup — running `kubectl get svc -n dev-ethan` yields TLS handshake error: "x509: certificate signed by unknown authority". I copied the kubeconfig from Vault as raw yaml.

devex: that could be the cluster CA missing in the kubeconfig; when we mint SA configs we inject cluster.caData. Did you copy via the CLI helper or paste manually? If manual, try the CLI helper link from the request page to download the pre-baked file.

eng-infra: rechecked KUBE-REQ-2399 — looks like the download blob had CRLF introduced by the browser copy path. I've regenerated and re-uploaded, pls re-download. Also note there's a macOS `kubectl` plugin that sometimes strips certs when you `kubectl config view --flatten` so avoid flatten for now.

Ethan Park: re-downloaded, `kubectl get svc -n dev-ethan` now works. port-forward succeeded and my app is reachable at localhost:8080. thanks!

devex: nice. we'll add a short note in the quickstart about the CRLF/paste issue and the flatten plugin. if you need CI service account access later, open a new request and tag infra. enjoy day 1! :rocket:

title: #devex: 1790123456
preview:
Nina Gomez: Heads up — after updating to redwood-inference-js@2.2.0 our SPA bundles jumped ~30KB and a few edge routes started failing with "TypeError: client.retry is not a function" in prod only. Local dev (pnpm dev) is fine.

Ethan Park: that sounds like an ESM/CJS interop issue combined with tree-shaking. Did you change how you import the SDK?

Nina Gomez: we changed to `import * as RW from 'redwood-inference-js'` to avoid ts2322 warnings. Then we do `const client = new RW.default({ apiKey })`.

Theo Martins: `import * as pkg` + `.default` is brittle across bundlers — sometimes the default export is a wrapper. Also if retry is attached to the prototype in a separate file and that file was flagged as side-effect-only it can be removed by tree-shaking.

Nina Gomez: looking at node_modules/redwood-inference-js/dist, the retry logic lives in `dist/strategies/retry.cjs.js` and package.json has "sideEffects": false. The ESM bundle imports it via `require('./strategies/retry.cjs.js')` behind a conditional. Maybe the bundler dropped the require branch?

Priya Rao: edge runtimes often use esbuild-ish tree-shaking in Vite. If the conditional require is not statically analyzable, bundlers sometimes prune it. Also mixing CJS files inside an ESM package increases duplication (esbuild may include a small CJS runtime wrapper). That could explain the +30KB.

Ethan Park: quick repro steps: build the app with `pnpm build` and inspect `.next/server/chunks` (or Vite output) for duplicated retry code. Also run `npx source-map-explorer` or `vite build --analyze` to spot the added files.

Nina Gomez: ran analyzer: found `retry.cjs.js` present in node_modules and a bundled copy ended up in _another_ chunk as `vendor_retry.js` (duplicated wrapper code). The runtime error is because client instance's prototype doesn't include retry — looks like the SDK's ESM entrypoint conditionally assigns exports based on env and the bundler chose a path that omitted prototype patching.

Theo Martins: probable SDK fixes:
- avoid conditional require/require-from-esm patterns; use pure ESM entrypoints with static imports so bundlers see usage
- mark necessary files as side-effects in package.json (e.g. "sideEffects": ["./dist/strategies/retry.cjs.js"] ) if they need to run for initialization
- unify the single class/prototype implementation instead of patching via separate module side-effects
- export named helpers so apps can import `import { initRetries } from 'redwood-inference-js/strategies'` explicitly

Priya Rao: app workaround for now: explicitly import the retry module at top level: `import 'redwood-inference-js/dist/strategies/retry.cjs.js'`. That forces bundlers to keep it and avoids the prototype missing. Downside: extra bytes and fragile path coupling to dist layout.

Nina Gomez: we tried explicit import — fixes the runtime error and deduplicates (surprisingly the bundler then reuses the single copy). Bundle size drops back down ~12KB relative to the broken build.

Ethan Park: another app-side trick: pin to the previous SDK patch that had unified ESM entrypoint, or use `resolve.alias` to point imports to the CJS build to avoid interop paths. Not ideal long-term.

Theo Martins: SDK unit/integration tests should include a tiny consumer build with Vite and Next edge to catch these regressions. Also add a changelog note about bundlers and sideEffects.

Nina Gomez: opened internal issue RW-4477 and left reproduction steps + build artifacts: https://redwood.internal/issue/RW-4477

DevEx Bot: Created ticket RW-4477 for SDK: "Conditional require + sideEffects:false causes retry module to be tree-shaken -> runtime errors + bundle bloat" — https://redwood.internal/issue/RW-4477 :spiral_note_pad:

Priya Rao: tagging @eng-runtime and @devex for visibility. Any chance SDK could publish an alternate entry (like "exports.{ \"import\": \"./dist/esm/edge.mjs\" }") that keeps everything static?

Theo Martins: yes — exports map with separate entrypoints for `import`, `require`, and `edge` consumers is best. Also consider an opt-in `sideEffects: true` for a minor release to be safe while refactoring.

Nina Gomez: thanks all. We'll keep the explicit import in our app and wait for SDK patch. Leaving notes in onboarding doc about CJS/ESM pitfalls. :memo:

title: #customer-success: 1780003456
preview:
Alex Kim: quick sync — we need a short customer-facing RCA and timeline for ACME. They saw degraded embeddings for ~45m yesterday. Can we draft a safe summary and a follow-up plan? @Maria you own comms, @Devon please add what happened on call.
IncidentBot: Incident 2026-03-14-EMB-42 created by oncall_devon at 2026-03-14T02:40:12Z — severity: high. Paging: dev-oncall, eng-sre, customer-success.
Devon Patel: tl;dr — KV-cache corruption on a dedicated pool led to 5–25s tail latencies for embedding requests. Happened after a rolling kernel upgrade on the host fleet; one node failed to clear stale cache pointers so some inference workers served stale pointers -> OOM-ish behavior and long GC stalls. The autoscaler mitigated after 18m and we evicted the bad hosts manually.
Sophie Chen: adding details — kernel upgrade triggered a rare libnuma reorder. Repro locally with a stress runner and exact kernel + drm drivers. We rolled a hotfix that prevents the cache pointer reuse and forced a restart on affected hosts. Patch PR: https://git.internal/redwood/infra/commit/abc123
Devon Patel: timeline (local notes):
- 02:34 UTC: alert — embedding p95 > 2s, errors spike
- 02:36 UTC: oncall paged, started triage
- 02:45 UTC: identified hosts with repeated mem faults, isolated
- 02:58 UTC: autoscaler evicts/replicates; partial recovery starts
- 03:12 UTC: manual host drain and restart completed
- 03:19 UTC: monitoring shows latency back to baseline; closing incident
Maria Lopez: Draft customer message (short + non-technical):
"Hi ACME team — we want to follow up on the embedding degradation you experienced on 2026-03-14. Briefly: a software update on our host fleet caused a small subset of hosts to behave incorrectly, which slowed some embedding requests for about 45 minutes. We identified the issue, rolled a fix, and your workload recovered. There is no evidence of data loss. We will: 1) ensure the kernel update process includes this driver check, 2) add canary staging to that patch path, and 3) alert you if we see related anomalies. If you want a deeper technical RCA or a call, let us know and we can schedule. — Maria, Customer Success"
Ethan Park: ACME contact is OK with a short note — they asked for ETA on permanent mitigation and whether we can add a Dedicated health check for them.
Sophie Chen: Permanent mitigation plan (engineering phrasing for CS):
- Backport hotfix to kernel-update orchestration to skip nodes with the conflicting driver version.
- Add an automated preflight that runs a lightweight KV-cache integrity check after kernel change and before returning nodes to the pool.
- Add observable metric: kv_cache_pointer_reuse_count and an alert at >0 for 1m.
- Run a targeted canary (5% of capacity) for 24h on future host updates.
Devon Patel: Customer-impact assessment: Affected percentage of ACME requests ~3.8% during window; most requests were slightly delayed, <1% saw transient errors that retried successfully. No data exfiltration or model corruption.
Maria Lopez: Proposed status page update (short):
"We experienced a partial degradation of embedding latency for some customers on 2026-03-14 between 02:34–03:19 UTC due to a host-level software update. Services have recovered. Root cause: host kernel/driver interaction causing KV-cache instability. Remediation: host drains and a hotfix; longer-term mitigations in progress. For customers who saw impact, CS will follow up individually." — do we want to add the incident link?

title: #announcements: 1771234802
preview:
Marissa Cole: :tada: Auto-rollback on SLO breach (MVP) is now available for INTERNAL opt-in (behind a feature flag).

What it does (MVP):
• During canary/A-B/staged rollouts, we evaluate latency + error-rate SLO burn-rate for the rollout scope (route + model version + region + tier).
• If we detect a breach per policy, controller will auto-*pause* the rollout (default) or auto-*rollback* (if enabled in policy).
• Every decision is recorded in the rollout audit trail + appears in Console.

What it does NOT do (yet):
• Multi-region failover orchestration
• Quality/eval-based rollback signals

Docs / links:
• PRD (internal): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/PROD/Auto-rollback+on+SLO+breach+integration
• Runbook (oncall + service owners): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENGSRE/Auto-rollback+on+SLO+breach+runbook
• Metrics naming + labels: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ARCH/Rollouts+SLO+metrics+naming+and+labeling
• Default thresholds + templates (PM draft): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/redwood/console-rollouts/auto-rollback-policy-options
• Dashboards/alerts PR (Grafana pack): https://github.com/observability-pack/pull/913

How to opt in (staging first; prod after SRE sign-off):
1) Confirm you have SLO monitors in slo-toolkit for:
   • p95 latency (fast+slow burn)
   • error rate (5xx)
   Monitors must be scoped to your rollout dimensions (route/model version/region). If you’re missing coverage, reply here and we’ll route to owners.
2) Enable the feature flag for your service:
   • `rollouts.auto_rollback.enabled=true` (service-level)
   • (Optional) `rollouts.auto_rollback.allow_rollback=true` (if you want rollback vs pause)
3) Add policy section to your rollout config (example below) OR use a template in Console Policy Editor (new section: “Auto-rollback (SLO)”).

Policy snippet (example):
```json
{
  "autoRollback": {
    "enabled": true,
    "action": "PAUSE",
    "signals": ["LATENCY_P95", "ERROR_RATE"],
    "windows": {"fast": "5m", "slow": "30m"},
    "debounce": "10m",
    "minRequests": 2000,
    "suppressDuringIncident": true
  }
}
```

Guardrails worth calling out:
• Minimum volume gate (avoids false positives on low traffic)
• Dual-window confirmation (fast+slow burn)
• Debounce/holdoff to prevent oscillation
• If monitors are stale/missing, controller will NOT rollback; it will log `monitor_missing_or_stale` and keep rollout in current state (and we’ll alert).

If you want to opt in, reply with:
• service + route
• model(s) + region(s)
• tier (hosted/dedicated/private)
• desired action (PAUSE vs ROLLBACK)
• owner + oncall channel

Owners for questions:
• Product/policy: @Paula Mendes
• Rollout controller: @Jada Williams / @Chloe Martin
• SLO semantics + oncall readiness: @Sean Gallagher / @Rafael Mendes

Thank you—this is a big reliability milestone.

Paula Mendes: Adding: Console UI is behind the same flag. If you don’t see the “Auto-rollback (SLO)” section in Policy Editor, ping me + include your org/service.

Jada Williams: Small clarification: rollback action currently uses a step-down strategy for traffic split (rate-limited) + is idempotent, but we’re still staging it per-service. If you’re the first prod adopter, expect tighter coordination.

Sean Gallagher: SRE note: for prod enablement we require (a) runbook link in your service doc, (b) dashboard access for oncall, (c) monitors verified non-stale for 7 days. We’ll do a quick 15m sign-off.

Aisha Bello: Dashboards are landing today; alerting is notify-only by default (no pages) until we tune thresholds. If your service opts in, we’ll add routing to the right Slack channel.

Connor O'Brien: Release note: feature flag defaults OFF in prod. Staging enablement is self-serve; prod enablement goes through ENG-4850 checklist. If you need help flipping flags, open a request in #eng-releases.

deploy-bot: Observability pack updated in staging: `observability-pack@v2.31.0` (includes rollout auto-rollback dashboards)

Felix Schneider: Audit trail: Console shows an “Auto action” event with decision outcome + suppression reason + links to monitor defs. If you’re missing links/fields, file a bug + include rollout_id.

Rafael Mendes: We’re running a game day next week (latency spike, 5xx burst, stale monitor). If your team wants to participate as a pilot adopter, reply and we’ll slot you in.

Marissa Cole: Thanks all. Pilot target: 2–3 internal services in staging this week, then 1 internal prod route next week. We’ll post follow-ups here with known issues / tuning changes.

Ethan Park: FYI: policy validation will reject unsafe configs (e.g., debounce < 5m, minRequests < 500). Error messages should point to the exact field.

Allison Grant: Please do NOT add free-form labels to the new metrics. Stick to the standard label set in the doc (cardinality limits). If you need an extra dimension, ask in #eng-sre first.

incident-bot: Reminder: auto-rollback events will post to Slack once incident-bot PR-477 is deployed to prod. Current staging notifications go to #eng-sre-test.

Paula Mendes: FAQ I’m getting in DMs: yes, you can set per-rollout overrides (e.g., stricter for a risky model version) and keep service defaults otherwise. UI will show both + which one won.

Chloe Martin: One more note: if controller can’t fetch SLO status (toolkit timeout), it will treat as “no signal” and log it. We’re tracking rates of this; please report if you see it frequently.

Marissa Cole: If you’re replying to opt in, please include your preferred window for enablement (this week vs next) so we can manage support load.

title: #eng-platform: 1769984123
preview:
Jada Williams: Kicking off the spike thread. We need to decide *where* to hook SLO evaluation into the rollout state machine. Question: do we evaluate inside the controller loop (before advancing steps) or as an async sidecar / separate worker that can pause/rollback via API?

Chloe Martin: +1. From Console side we mainly need consistent semantics + auditability. But for the actual hook, I’d prefer controller-owned so it’s deterministic w/ step transitions.

Ethan Park: Control plane POV: if controller owns evaluation, it must not block step transitions on slow monitor queries. We’ll need timeouts + “monitor missing/stale” behavior spelled out.

Benji Okafor: Also policy engine needs to supply defaults/override per rollout. If evaluation happens in controller, it needs to fetch a resolved policy bundle at runtime (and handle policy versioning).

Jordan Lee: State machine option: evaluate at the boundary between STEP_OBSERVE and STEP_ADVANCE. That way we can enforce: “don’t increase traffic if fast burn is tripped”, “rollback if both windows trip”, etc.

Hannah Rivera: Where does that live in code? In the `advanceRollout()` path we currently do: compute next step -> persist -> executor. We can insert a guard before the persist.

Jada Williams: Yup. Current controller tick:
- load rollout
- reconcile desired step
- if needs transition: write state + emit event
- executor acts
We can add `EvaluateSafety()` before any transition that increases blast radius.

Logan Wright: Telemetry note: evaluation will need request volume + timestamps, not just burn rate boolean. Otherwise we’ll flap / false positive on low volume. So hook should have access to sample size gating.

Rafael Mendes: From SRE, I’d like hook *before* any traffic shift up, and also during steady-state “observe” windows. If it’s only on step change, you can miss a regression that happens mid-step.

Jada Williams: Good call. We can do both:
1) periodic evaluation while in any “active rollout” state
2) hard gate on transitions that increase traffic split

Chloe Martin: If periodic evaluation triggers an action (pause/rollback), we need to surface it in Console timeline. Felix, can you sanity check audit event needs?

Felix Schneider: For sure. If controller triggers, it should emit a structured audit event with: rollout_id, previous_state, action (PAUSE/ROLLBACK), reason, monitor ids, metrics snapshot ref, policy version, correlation id. (Aligning w/ ADR draft.)

Ethan Park: Re: where to call policy engine: controller already pulls rollout config, but policy engine is separate. I’d propose: controller asks policy service for “resolved auto-rollback policy” keyed by (service/route/model/region/tier + rollout overrides). Cache for ~30s.

Benji Okafor: +1. We can provide a single endpoint returning a resolved JSON blob + `policy_version` + TTL. Controller can pass that into evaluator.

Jordan Lee: Concretely, I’m thinking an interface in controller:
```
type SafetyEvaluator interface {
  Evaluate(ctx, rollout) (Decision, error)
}

type Decision struct {
  Outcome string // OK | PAUSE | ROLLBACK | SUPPRESS
  Reason string
  Details string // compact for logs
}
```
Adapter to slo-toolkit handles fetching fast/slow burn + sample + staleness.

Hannah Rivera: If Evaluate() errors (monitor provider down), what do we do? Fail-open (continue rollout) seems risky; fail-closed (pause) can block deploys during telemetry outages.

Rafael Mendes: SRE preference: fail-*safe* but not fail-closed always. I think: if monitor missing/stale -> SUPPRESS + alert + do not auto-rollback. But also gate “increase traffic” unless explicitly allowed (flag?)

Jada Williams: That’s basically “pause on unknown” for traffic increases, but don’t automatically rollback on unknown. I like it.

Marissa Cole: Jumping in (PM). MVP requirement: if we can’t confidently evaluate, we should not rollback automatically. Pause is acceptable, but we need a clear Console message: “paused due to missing/invalid SLO monitor”.

Chloe Martin: Works. We can map to a new rollout status reason code in Console.

Ethan Park: Implementation detail: controller uses optimistic locking in step transitions. If evaluator runs every tick, ensure we don’t spam pause events. Needs debounce/holdoff.

Jordan Lee: We can store `last_slo_eval_ts`, `last_slo_eval_outcome`, and `last_auto_action_ts` on the rollout record. Decision engine can enforce holdoff like 10m after an action.

Logan Wright: And please emit a metric per evaluation result with low-card labels. Something like `rollout_slo_eval_total{outcome,service,region}` and avoid rollout_id as a label (cardinality). Link rollout_id via logs/audit event.

Jada Williams: +1 no rollout_id label. Audit stream is where id lives.

Hannah Rivera: Where exactly in state machine should we call Evaluate()?

Jada Williams: Proposal:
- On each tick, if rollout.state in {CANARY_ACTIVE, AB_ACTIVE, STAGED_ACTIVE} -> Evaluate()
- If Decision == PAUSE or ROLLBACK -> transition to PAUSED/ROLLING_BACK (idempotent)
- Additionally, before any transition that increases traffic (e.g. 10%->25%): Evaluate() and require OK.

Benji Okafor: Edge: what about transitions that *decrease* traffic (rollback executor)? Should not be blocked by evaluator.

Jada Williams: Correct. Decreases should be allowed even if telemetry is busted.

Ethan Park: How does controller know “increase traffic”? Today it just applies step configs. We can add a helper comparing previous split vs next split.

Jordan Lee: Something like `IsMoreRisky(prev, next) bool` based on traffic weight, region expansion, tier expansion, etc.

Chloe Martin: Please include model version change as “more risky” too. Some steps are 0% -> 0% but flip version for internal shadow, etc.

Jada Williams: Yep. Risk dimension set should include:
- traffic_weight
- customer_tier exposure
- region count
- model_version exposure

Felix Schneider: Also: when evaluation triggers action, attach a “decision trace” string, even if it’s compact. Helps in UI “why did we pause?” panel.

Rafael Mendes: Can you add a TODO for staleness detection? We saw in previous incidents: metrics lag ~2-3m. We should treat “last datapoint older than N minutes” as unknown.

Logan Wright: We can get `as_of` timestamp from slo-toolkit query. Define stale if `now - as_of > 180s` (tunable). Put it in the resolved policy defaults.

Benji Okafor: OK, policy engine can set default: `max_data_age_seconds: 180` and safe bounds [60, 900].

Marissa Cole: Sounds good. Can we capture a clear next step? Spike output should be: where hook lives + API shape + decisioning semantics.

Jada Williams: Next steps:
- Jada/Hannah: implement skeleton `SafetyEvaluator` hook in controller behind `AUTO_ROLLBACK_SLO_EVAL` flag (pause-only first)
- Ethan/Benji/Jordan: draft resolved policy endpoint + schema fields needed by evaluator
- Logan: confirm metrics + logging guidance (no rollout_id labels)
- Chloe/Felix: list UI/audit fields needed so we don’t have to refactor later

Chloe Martin: :thumbsup: I’ll start a short doc for Console requirements and link it here.

Ethan Park: :thumbsup: Will sync w/ Benji/Jordan on policy endpoint. We’ll aim for a minimal contract for the spike.

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup: I’ll drop staleness + fail-open/closed recommendation in this thread after looking at past telemetry outages.

Jada Williams: Great. I’ll post a draft PR by EOD with the hook location (even if evaluator returns OK always).

Hannah Rivera: If Evaluate() errors (monitor provider down), what do we do? Fail-open (continue rollout) seems risky; fail-closed (pause) can block deploys during telemetry outages.

Rafael Mendes: SRE preference: fail-*safe* but not fail-closed always. I think: if monitor missing/stale -> SUPPRESS + alert + do not auto-rollback. But also gate “increase traffic” unless explicitly allowed (flag?)

Jada Williams: That’s basically “pause on unknown” for traffic increases, but don’t automatically rollback on unknown. I like it.

Marissa Cole: Jumping in (PM). MVP requirement: if we can’t confidently evaluate, we should not rollback automatically. Pause is acceptable, but we need a clear Console message: “paused due to missing/invalid SLO monitor”.

Chloe Martin: Works. We can map to a new rollout status reason code in Console.

Ethan Park: Implementation detail: controller uses optimistic locking in step transitions. If evaluator runs every tick, ensure we don’t spam pause events. Needs debounce/holdoff.

Jordan Lee: We can store `last_slo_eval_ts`, `last_slo_eval_outcome`, and `last_auto_action_ts` on the rollout record. Decision engine can enforce holdoff like 10m after an action.

Logan Wright: And please emit a metric per evaluation result with low-card labels. Something like `rollout_slo_eval_total{outcome,service,region}` and avoid rollout_id as a label (cardinality). Link rollout_id via logs/audit event.

Jada Williams: +1 no rollout_id label. Audit stream is where id lives.

Hannah Rivera: Where exactly in state machine should we call Evaluate()?

Jada Williams: Proposal:
- On each tick, if rollout.state in {CANARY_ACTIVE, AB_ACTIVE, STAGED_ACTIVE} -> Evaluate()
- If Decision == PAUSE or ROLLBACK -> transition to PAUSED/ROLLING_BACK (idempotent)
- Additionally, before any transition that increases traffic (e.g. 10%->25%): Evaluate() and require OK.

Benji Okafor: Edge: what about transitions that *decrease* traffic (rollback executor)? Should not be blocked by evaluator.

Jada Williams: Correct. Decreases should be allowed even if telemetry is busted.

Ethan Park: How does controller know “increase traffic”? Today it just applies step configs. We can add a helper comparing previous split vs next split.

Jordan Lee: Something like `IsMoreRisky(prev, next) bool` based on traffic weight, region expansion, tier expansion, etc.

Chloe Martin: Please include model version change as “more risky” too. Some steps are 0% -> 0% but flip version for internal shadow, etc.

Jada Williams: Yep. Risk dimension set should include:
- traffic_weight
- customer_tier exposure
- region count
- model_version exposure

Felix Schneider: Also: when evaluation triggers action, attach a “decision trace” string, even if it’s compact. Helps in UI “why did we pause?” panel.

Rafael Mendes: Can you add a TODO for staleness detection? We saw in previous incidents: metrics lag ~2-3m. We should treat “last datapoint older than N minutes” as unknown.

Logan Wright: We can get `as_of` timestamp from slo-toolkit query. Define stale if `now - as_of > 180s` (tunable). Put it in the resolved policy defaults.

Benji Okafor: OK, policy engine can set default: `max_data_age_seconds: 180` and safe bounds [60, 900].

Marissa Cole: Sounds good. Can we capture a clear next step? Spike output should be: where hook lives + API shape + decisioning semantics.

Jada Williams: Next steps:
- Jada/Hannah: implement skeleton `SafetyEvaluator` hook in controller behind `AUTO_ROLLBACK_SLO_EVAL` flag (pause-only first)
- Ethan/Benji/Jordan: draft resolved policy endpoint + schema fields needed by evaluator
- Logan: confirm metrics + logging guidance (no rollout_id labels)
- Chloe/Felix: list UI/audit fields needed so we don’t have to refactor later

Chloe Martin: :thumbsup: I’ll start a short doc for Console requirements and link it here.

Ethan Park: :thumbsup: Will sync w/ Benji/Jordan on policy endpoint. We’ll aim for a minimal contract for the spike.

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup: I’ll drop staleness + fail-open/closed recommendation in this thread after looking at past telemetry outages.

Jada Williams: Great. I’ll post a draft PR by EOD with the hook location (even if evaluator returns OK always).

title: #eng-ml: 1769267448
preview:
Aditya Rao: hey folks, quick q on Cost Model what-if endpoint. Optimize wants to add a param for `cache_warm_rate` (0..1) so we can model “cold start” vs “mostly warm” prefix/KV cache. otherwise ROI for caching recs is super misleading for customers with bursty traffic.

Hassan Mirza: +1. right now in our prototype we assume warm cache after N requests, but that’s basically hand-wavy. would be much better if what-if can take a warm-rate or “expected hit ratio” so we can present savings bands.

Jonas Weber: to clarify: not asking for full simulator. just a single knob that scales cache-hit contribution in the breakdown. ex: warm_rate=0.2 means only 20% of eligible tokens get the cache discount.

Ethan Park: concern: adding an unconstrained knob makes the API non-deterministic in practice (and easy to game). we’re trying to standardize a small set of canonical inputs that reflect real observed system state, not guesses.

Aditya Rao: understood, but we need *some* way to represent burstiness / cache churn. otherwise our “enable prefix cache” rec always looks like 30–40% savings, and then CS gets escalations when customers see ~5%.

Thomas Baird: also from perf side: every extra dimension on what-if inputs increases cardinality and makes it harder to cache responses safely (Console + Optimize will hit this a lot). we already have strict bounds in the guardrails PR.

Hassan Mirza: what about treating warm-rate as optional hint, with defaults + bounds (e.g. 0, 0.5, 0.9 only)? we can expose it as a small enum.

Ethan Park: if it’s an enum, we still need semantics. is it “probability a request hits warm cache”? is it “fraction of tokens eligible”? those aren’t equivalent.

Logan Wright: from telemetry side we *can* estimate observed cache hit rate by route/model over a window (with lag). if Optimize wants to run what-if, we could take a workload summary input that includes historical hit_rate + confidence, instead of a user-supplied knob.

Jonas Weber: historical hit_rate doesn’t help for “new feature launch” where there’s no baseline yet.

Dr. Maya Srinivasan: stepping back: Optimize needs to communicate uncertainty. if platform won’t accept a knob, could the response include a sensitivity analysis? like cost for {cold, mixed, warm} scenarios computed server-side.

Thomas Baird: that’s closer to what we’d accept: bounded scenarios computed by the service, not arbitrary floats coming from clients. otherwise we’ll get 10k unique warm_rate values and no caching.

Aditya Rao: could we do something like:
- request includes `cache_state = COLD | MIXED | WARM`
- MIXED maps to a standard assumption (say 0.5)
- service returns the assumption used in response metadata

Ethan Park: I like that better. but we still need to define “MIXED” in the taxonomy doc and keep it stable across versions.

Sofia Ivanova: also note: “warm” depends on KV cache vs prefix cache vs paged-attn cache behavior. a single scalar might mislead unless it’s explicitly scoped to *prefix cache eligibility* (or whichever cache we’re modeling in v1).

Hassan Mirza: good call. for v1 our rec is specifically prefix caching at the router layer (not KV reuse across turns). so the knob can be “prefix_cache_warm_state” only.

Logan Wright: if you scope it to prefix cache, we can later backfill with telemetry-derived metrics for “observed prefix cache warmness” and reconcile.

Ethan Park: proposal:
1) no free-form `cache_warm_rate` float in v1
2) add optional `prefix_cache_warm_state` enum (COLD/MIXED/WARM) *or* we compute and return 3 scenarios by default for cache-related what-if
3) document exact meaning: fraction of eligible requests expected to be served from prefix cache within the modeled window

Aditya Rao: would Optimize be allowed to select the scenario (e.g. customer says “we’re mostly bursty” -> choose MIXED) and display the other two as bounds?

Thomas Baird: yes, and it keeps request space bounded. we can also limit scenario computation to only when `what_if` includes cache changes.

Jonas Weber: works for us. we’ll update the rec UX to show a range and call out the assumption.

Dr. Maya Srinivasan: please also include a field in response like `assumptions.prefix_cache_warm_state = MIXED` and maybe `confidence = LOW` if there’s no baseline.

Ethan Park: ok. can you file an ENG ticket for the enum + semantics? and link it to the guardrails work so we don’t miss it.

Aditya Rao: will do. I’ll draft in the Optimize working doc and open a ticket today.

Hassan Mirza: thanks all. :thumbsup:

Ethan Park: :thumbsup: (and please avoid calling it “warm rate” in customer-facing strings; it reads like we’re measuring exact cache hit % when we’re really modeling a scenario.)

title: #eng: 1768625500
preview:
Dylan Brooks: heads up — we found duplicate event emission on `signup_completed` + `activation_first_request` (looks like it fires twice on some redirect paths). this is skewing the activation funnel + email trigger cohorts. quick patch PR is up, can we get fast reviews?

Dylan Brooks: PR: https://github.com/redwood/redwood/pull/28521 ("Fix duplicate signup event emission")

Dylan Brooks: repro is basically: sign up -> verify email -> back button -> continue -> we emit twice. also seeing it from retry logic on the client.

Dylan Brooks: logs from prod sample (same `event_id` missing, so both pass through):
```
[events] name=signup_completed user_id=usr_8c1... session_id=sess_33a... request_id=req_01HT... ts=...
[events] name=signup_completed user_id=usr_8c1... session_id=sess_33a... request_id=req_01HT... ts=... (+120ms)
```

Nadia Rahman: :eyes: taking a look. do we have an idempotency key now or just dedupe on server by (user_id, name, window)?

Dylan Brooks: server-side dedupe in the ingestion layer. PR adds a `client_event_uuid` + also a short window dedupe for legacy clients. (we can’t rely on request_id bc it can change)

Logan Wright: +1 we need this asap — dashboard is showing weird >100% conversions in a couple cohorts. what’s the dedupe window?

Dylan Brooks: 10s window keyed on (user_id, event_name, client_event_uuid if present else session_id + path). only for the handful of onboarding events.

Ethan Park: reviewing now. does this affect downstream billing/metering or just lifecycle analytics?

Dylan Brooks: just product analytics + lifecycle triggers. metering uses token usage events so no direct $ impact, but it does affect “first_success” segments.

Mei Lin: data side: we also saw this in the warehouse — duplicates started around the console redirect change last week. if this lands today, we should annotate the funnel dataset for the spike window.

Allison Grant: +1 on annotation. also please confirm we’re not dropping legitimate retries (e.g., offline -> resend) with the 10s window.

Dylan Brooks: yep, legitimate retries should have different `client_event_uuid` once SDK update is in. for legacy, 10s is conservative; the duplicates we see are within <1s.

Jada Williams: quick question: how does this interact with experiment cohort assignment? if cohort is set on signup event and we dedupe, are we stable?

Dylan Brooks: cohort assignment happens earlier (server-side at account create). these events are just emitting + used for funnel stages and email triggers.

Benji Okafor: can we ship this behind a flag in ingestion? in case we need to roll back quickly.

Dylan Brooks: PR includes a kill switch: `EVENT_DEDUPE_ONBOARDING_ENABLED` default on for 10% then ramp.

DeployBot: Buildkite build #118402 for PR #28521 started. (<https://buildkite.com/redwood/redwood/builds/118402|view>)

Nadia Rahman: left 2 minor comments (naming + add `source=console` to dedupe key for one event). otherwise LGTM.

Ethan Park: approved. only nit: can we log when we dedupe (count metric) so we can validate drop post-deploy?

Dylan Brooks: good call — added `events.deduped.count` metric + tags (event_name, source). pushed.

DeployBot: Buildkite build #118402 passed. (<https://buildkite.com/redwood/redwood/builds/118402|view>)

Logan Wright: approved from analytics side. pls ping when deployed so we can re-run the sanity queries.

Dylan Brooks: merged. going to request hotfix deploy to prod console + ingestion today if release manager ok.

Benji Okafor: I can take the deploy. what’s the target window?

Dylan Brooks: next 1–2 hrs ideally (before the next email send batch). also FYI marketing asked for confirmation since triggers were noisy.

DeployBot: PR #28521 merged into main by Dylan Brooks.

Allison Grant: once it’s out, I’ll update the activation dashboard annotation + alert thresholds (the duplicates were tripping the “activation jump” alert).

Mei Lin: I’ll backfill a deduped view for the affected date range for the readout, but long-term should be fine once fixed.

Dylan Brooks: thanks all. Benji lmk when deploy is complete; I’ll ping Logan/Nadia for validation.

Benji Okafor: deployed to ingestion at 1768629042. ramped to 10% and watching dedupe metric now.
